description_long: This is an irregularly shaped, medium-sized stone with a rugged, angular profile and a predominantly yellowish-brown coloration. The surface is uneven, featuring a mix of rough, jagged textures and some smoother, weathered patches, with visible striations and natural layering. Subtle variations in color, including lighter beige and off-white streaks, suggest mineral inclusions or sedimentary banding. The stone appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or artificial modifications. Its size and form make it suitable as a natural obstacle or decorative element in urban landscapes such as parks or roadside verges. The overall appearance indicates exposure to the elements, with minor surface erosion and discoloration.
description: A rugged, yellowish-brown stone with jagged surfaces, natural striations, and subtle color variations. Its irregular shape and weathered texture suggest it has been exposed to the elements.
description_view_0: This view highlights the broad, uneven face of the stone, showing prominent striations and a mix of rough and smoother areas.
description_view_1: The stone appears narrower and more elongated from this angle, emphasizing its layered structure and vertical ridges.
description_view_2: A side profile reveals the stone's tapering form and the transition between rough and smoother surfaces, with visible color gradation.
description_view_3: This end view shows a blockier, more compact aspect, with pronounced layering and a relatively flat surface.
attribute_location: urban park edge or roadside verge
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or partially embedded in the ground
attribute_pattern: Natural striations and subtle sedimentary layering, with irregular mottling and color variation
attribute_weathering: Moderately weathered with rough, eroded surfaces and some smoother, discolored patches
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with soil, gravel, or adjacent stones; may contrast with paved surfaces or manicured lawns
